learning: Homebrew Python 3.14 pyexpat can break pre-commit remote Python hook environment creation before repo hooks run; local language: system hooks avoided that failing layer while preserving checks.

summary: Bootstrap chrome plugins run in a different JS scope than the test env. Bugs like 'Node is not defined' (chrome scope lacks DOM globals like Node) and Xray-wrapper underscore-property hiding (event.reader._iframe) only manifest in real Zotero, not test env. The e2e harness's own use of _dispatchEvent bypassed Zotero's customEvent bridge, so the harness saw 'fix works' while real Zotero saw bug. Mitigation: 3-tier fallback for chrome lookups (wrappedJSObject -> direct -> DOM-search) AND switching harness to dispatch via real customEvent bridge.

mcp__codex__codex is callable from the orchestrator session but DENIED from subagent contexts (even with sandbox:read-only). Forge code-review iterations must be driven directly from the orchestrator, not delegated to a 'codex-tests' subagent. Costs context but unavoidable.

BUG-AC8-1 class: provider adapters that read happy-path body fields and yield message_end on stream end mask upstream errors (HTTP 4xx/5xx with error JSON). Fix is to detect terminal error chunks (top-level error key OR done_reason==='error') and yield in-band error event. Recurred at proxy layer until iter-2 codex review caught it again.

new URL('/api/tags', baseUrl) STRIPS path prefixes (e.g. localhost:11400/codex becomes localhost:11400/api/tags), causing the wrong endpoint to be probed. Always concatenate: trimmedBase + '/api/tags'. Caught when user combined /codex URL with gemma model and validator wrongly accepted.

Parallel agent dispatch (~3-5 at once) is highly productive but requires explicit file-scope partitioning in each agent's brief. Without it, agents step on bootstrap.ts and settings-view.ts. Pattern: 'orchestrator does the final integration; subagents export wireX() functions that orchestrator wires in bootstrap.'

Settings dialog rendered inputs for an entire session before anyone noticed: NO event handlers were ever attached. Users typed values that vanished on dialog close. Caught only by user manual smoke. Lesson: render+wire pattern needed an integration test that proves Save persists to prefs and reopen reads them back.

Codex code review caught 4 ACs that passed unit tests but did not deliver end-to-end: crawler supported Zotero.PDFWorker but bootstrap never threaded it; selection.source was populated but never put in the LLM prompt; openCitationInReader ignored the resolved attachmentKey. Unit tests with injected deps mask missing production wiring. Verify the bootstrap/runtime actually threads each dependency, not just that the consumer accepts it.

The plan HYPOTHESIZED the v0.2.0 e2e failures were an auto-reindex notifier race and mandated /rca-before-fix. /rca REFUTED it: real cause was the e2e driver using a global document.querySelector that hit a stale onboarding dialog, returning a detached button. Plan hypotheses about bug causation must be framed as hypotheses and verified by /rca before any fix is written.

The spec said '5 failing e2e tests since v0.2.0'; the actual CI run had 6 (AC3-scroll was missed). When scoping a 'fix the failing tests' AC, enumerate failures from the actual CI run log, not from a prose summary — the missed one (AC3-scroll) would have kept the release gate red.

rag-augmented-provider re-read + JSON.parse-d the entire 71MB per-provider index file on EVERY chat request (no cache), then ran cosine over all chunks — a multi-second tax per turn that surfaced only when the user reported 25s latency. Fixed with a memoized IndexStorage.read() keyed on a (meta-sidecar, primary stat) fingerprint. A size-only stat is not a sound change token — must include mtime or disable caching.
